column1: A subset \( W \subseteq V \) is a subspace if it is {{c1::non-empty}} and closed under {{c2::addition}} and {{c3::scalar multiplication}}
The dimension of \( \ker(T) \) plus the dimension of \( \text{im}(T) \) equals {{c1::the dimension of the domain}}

To show a set \( S = \{\mathbf{v}_1, \ldots, \mathbf{v}_k\} \) is linearly independent, show that {{c1::\( \alpha_1 \mathbf{v}_1 + \cdots + \alpha_k \mathbf{v}_k = \mathbf{0} \) implies \( \alpha_1 = \cdots = \alpha_k = 0 \)}}
column3: Check if columns of the matrix have non-trivial null space

column1: The {{c1::image}} of a linear map \( T: V \to W \) is the set \( \{ T(\mathbf{v}) : \mathbf{v} \in V \} \subseteq W \)
